natural_language_query: Find the top 5 users who have spent the most money specifically on 'Electronics', including their total spend and email address.
sql_query: SELECT u.email, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_electronics_spend FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.user_id = o.user_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id WHERE c.category_name = 'Electronics' AND o.status = 'Delivered' GROUP BY u.user_id, u.email ORDER BY total_electronics_spend DESC LIMIT 5;

natural_language_query: List all currently active users who have placed at least one order in the last 6 months but have NEVER bought anything from the 'Clothing' category.
sql_query: WITH ClothingBuyers AS (SELECT DISTINCT o.user_id FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.category_id WHERE c.category_name = 'Clothing') SELECT u.user_id, u.email, u.first_name, u.last_name FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.user_id = o.user_id WHERE u.is_active = TRUE AND o.order_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '6 months' AND u.user_id NOT IN (SELECT user_id FROM ClothingBuyers) GROUP BY u.user_id, u.email, u.first_name, u.last_name;

natural_language_query: Rank the top 3 best-selling products (by total quantity sold) within each individual category.
sql_query: WITH ProductSales AS (SELECT p.category_id, p.product_name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_sold, ROW_NUMBER() OVER(PARTITION BY p.category_id ORDER BY SUM(oi.quantity) DESC) as rank FROM products p JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.status != 'Pending' GROUP BY p.category_id, p.product_name) SELECT c.category_name, ps.product_name, ps.total_sold, ps.rank FROM ProductSales ps JOIN categories c ON ps.category_id = c.category_id WHERE ps.rank <= 3 ORDER BY c.category_name, ps.rank;

natural_language_query: What is the month-over-month total revenue growth for the year 2024?
sql_query: WITH MonthlyRevenue AS (SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date) AS order_month, SUM(total_amount) AS revenue FROM orders WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM order_date) = 2024 AND status = 'Delivered' GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date)) SELECT order_month, revenue, LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY order_month) AS previous_month_revenue, ROUND(((revenue - LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY order_month)) / LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY order_month)) * 100, 2) AS mom_growth_percentage FROM MonthlyRevenue ORDER BY order_month;

natural_language_query: Show me the average order value (AOV) and total number of orders for users, grouped by their signup year.
sql_query: SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM u.signup_date) AS signup_year, COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS total_orders, ROUND(AVG(o.total_amount), 2) AS average_order_value FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.user_id = o.user_id WHERE o.status != 'Pending' GROUP BY EXTRACT(YEAR FROM u.signup_date) ORDER BY signup_year DESC;
